info.set_name: Comprehensive Respiratory Set 1: Functional Anatomy (Short Answer)

q1.prompt: In the zero-to-23 generation model of the tracheobronchial tree, which generation is assigned to the trachea?.

q2.prompt: What are the physiological roles of the conducting zone regarding the physical properties of inspired air?.
correctOptionIds: To warm, humidify, and filter the air..

q3.prompt: By what cellular mechanism do the conducting airways remove inhaled particles that bypass the nasal filters?.
correctOptionIds: Via the beating of cilia which sweep mucus-trapped particles upwards..

q4.prompt: At which specific generation of the airway does the "respiratory zone" begin?.

q5.prompt: How is the anatomic dead space volume typically estimated for an adult based on their body weight?.
correctOptionIds: Approximately 1 millilitre per pound of body weight..

q6.prompt: According to the Bohr equation, how does the partial pressure of CO2  in mixed expired air compare to alveolar air when physiologic dead space is present?.
correctOptionIds: The partial pressure of CO2in expired air is lower than in alveolar air due to dilution by dead space air..

q7.prompt: Which three primary lung volumes must be summed to calculate the Vital Capacity?.
correctOptionIds: Tidal Volume (VT), Inspiratory Reserve Volume (IRV), and Expiratory Reserve Volume (ERV)..

q8.prompt: Why is the Functional Residual Capacity (FRC) defined as the equilibrium volume of the combined lung-chest wall system?.
correctOptionIds: Because the inward collapsing force of the lungs is exactly balanced by the outward expanding force of the chest wall..

q9.prompt: Which specific lung volume remains in the lungs after a maximal expiratory effort and cannot be measured by simple spirometry?.

q10.prompt: How does the calculation for Alveolar Ventilation (V˙A) differ from that of Minute Ventilation (V˙E ) in terms of "wasted" air?.
correctOptionIds: Alveolar Ventilation subtracts the dead space volume (VD) from each tidal volume (VT) before multiplying by the frequency..

q11.prompt: What is the definition of "Alveolar Dead Space," and in what clinical circumstance might it significantly increase?.
correctOptionIds: It refers to ventilated alveoli that do not participate in gas exchange, often due to a ventilation/perfusion defect like a pulmonary embolism..

q12.prompt: What is the approximate value for the partial pressure of CO2  in the anatomic dead space?.

q13.prompt: Which alveolar cell type is responsible for the synthesis of dipalmitoyl phosphatidylcholine?.

q14.prompt: What is the Respiratory Exchange Ratio (R), and what is its normal resting value?.
correctOptionIds: It is the ratio of CO2production to O2consumption, typically 0.8 at rest..

q15.prompt: What is the partial pressure of O2  in humidified tracheal air at 37°C at sea level?.
